commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Windows `mxc` delegated-run provider with config/env/flag support, provider registration, docs, and focused tests for MXC policy generation and provider setup.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for a feature PR; there is no bug reproduction path to establish. The runtime question is the pending real Windows MXC doctor and smoke proof described in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide when the preview MXC provider has enough live Windows proof to leave draft; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: feat(provider): add Windows MXC sandboxes
target.body:
## Summary
- add a one-shot local Windows `mxc` delegated-run provider
- generate deny-by-default network and checkout-scoped filesystem policies
- isolate temporary files and secret-bearing config under a current-user-only Windows ACL
- fail closed on host-DACL fallback, broad PATH exposure, script shims without `--shell`, and unsupported persistence
- add an execution-backed doctor check and document upstream preview/image constraints

## Validation
- `go test -race ./internal/providers/mxc ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.45.0 -test ./...`
- `GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build -trimpath -o /tmp/crabbox-mxc.exe ./cmd/crabbox`
- autoreview loop: all accepted adapter safety/correctness findings fixed; final PATH-toolchain finding consciously resolved in favor of fail-closed explicit `--mxc-readonly-paths` policy

## Live Windows findings
Validated on fresh Crabbox Azure Windows 11 24H2 (build 26100) and 25H2 (build 26200) VMs with Microsoft MXC 0.6.1 signed binaries. Provider registration, cross-built binary, build gating, and execution-backed doctor all work. Stock images are **not runnable yet**:

- `processcontainer` schema 0.6 fails because BaseContainer feature keys `61389575` and `61155944` are disabled even when MXC `--probe` reports the tier present
- schema 0.4 falls back to a BFS path unavailable in the signed production binary
- experimental `windows_sandbox` requires Windows Sandbox, host Python, companion binaries, and nested virtualization; after preparing those, the daemon hung beyond the configured five-minute TTL on Azure

This PR stays draft until an MXC-supported/prepared Windows image passes `crabbox doctor --provider mxc --target windows` and a real repo read/write + network-denial smoke. The provider intentionally fails closed meanwhile.
target.author: vincentkoc
